headline: Chip complex rebounds pre-open: Nasdaq futures +1.33%, S&P +0.49%, Dow +0.42% — Korea Jul 1-20 semi exports +181% YoY to $22.1B (record)
summary: US futures snap Monday's late-day fade with a chip-led bid. Korea semi exports for Jul 1-20 hit $22.1B, +180.6% YoY — a fresh record run rate driven by AI HBM/logic demand. Taiwan June export orders +59.4% YoY to $95.26B (vs +49.5% expected) reinforces the read. SOX/SMH set to reclaim last week's -10% drawdown as earnings-week visibility (GEV/IBM/GOOG/TSLA/INTC) begins.

headline: NVDA discloses 9.3% Nebius (NBIS) stake via 13G — 22.26M shares (mostly warrant from $2B March investment); NBIS +6.5% pre-market
summary: Nvidia filed Schedule 13G Jul 13 (public Jul 20) disclosing 9.3% beneficial ownership of Amsterdam-based neocloud Nebius: ~1.19M common + ~21M warrant shares tied to the $2B March 11, 2026 strategic investment (warrant not exercisable before Sep 11 2026). Reinforces Nvidia's 'preferred neocloud' playbook alongside CoreWeave — Jensen is happy to backstop capacity that consumes his GPUs. NBIS mkt cap ~$46B, +6.5% pre-market.

headline: TSMC to raise 2027 foundry prices 5-10% across advanced AND mature nodes; HPC over-forecast orders pay additional 10-15% premium
summary: Nikkei Asia reports TSMC finalized client price hikes taking effect January 2027: 5-10% baseline on both sub-7nm advanced (~74% of wafer revenue) and 28/16/12nm mature nodes. Beyond-forecast HPC orders (NVDA, AMD, AAPL, QCOM) face an extra 10-15% premium — some AI chip orders could see >20% total increase. Rationale cited: rising materials/equipment costs + overseas fab construction (Arizona, Kumamoto, Dresden). Confirms structural pricing power thesis; margin tailwind into 2027.

headline: Morgan Stanley software reset: MSFT assumed Overweight PT $600; CRM cut OW→EW PT $185 (from $287); ADBE to Underweight PT $240 (from $365); FTNT upgraded to EW PT $133 (from $80)
summary: Adam Wood-led coverage transfer redraws the AI-software winners map. MSFT ($600 PT) framed as the durable Copilot/Azure compounder. CRM slashed on 'Agentforce KPIs strong but not driving organic-growth inflection; legacy portfolio drag persists' — shares -3.6% pre-market. ADBE cut on generative-AI displacement risk to Creative Cloud/Firefly monetization. FTNT nearly doubled PT on cyber demand acceleration ahead of earnings season. Net-net: sell-side crowding into MSFT as the only 'safe' large-cap AI software name.

headline: Samsung Electronics (005930.KS) launches CEO-led 'RX Robotics' business office — hires ex-Hyundai/Boston Dynamics integration exec Lee Dongkun; stock +7.6% to KRW 262,500
summary: Samsung named robotics its next core growth pillar and stood up a Robotics eXperience (RX) Business Promotion Office reporting directly to the CEO — end-to-end control from strategy through key-tech development through commercialization. First deployments target humanoids for Samsung's own fab floors before consumer expansion. Lee Dongkun, who oversaw Hyundai's Boston Dynamics integration, hired to run strategy. KOSPI +4% on the print; SK Hynix outperformed as Mirae Asset top-1% traders net-bought hynix / net-sold Samsung on the physical-AI framing.

headline: Section 122 10% global tariff expires Thursday Jul 24 — administration pivots to Section 301/232; new tariff regime on 60+ trading partners could hit this week
summary: The Section 122 executive authority underpinning Trump's Feb 20 10% global baseline tariff sunsets Thu Jul 24 (180-day cap). Trade counsel widely expects a same-week pivot to Section 301 (unfair-trade) and Section 232 (national-security) authorities to reconstruct the tariff wall — USTR already proposed 10-12.5% on 60 trading partners over forced-labor allegations Jun 2. Brazil finalized at 25% Jul 15 as first target. Watch for a Wed-Thu Rose Garden print; risk-off tail if the replacement rate exceeds 10%.

headline: GE Vernova (GEV) Q2 pre-open Wed Jul 22: consensus EPS $3.23 / rev $10.7B; gas turbines booked into 2030, backlog + grid the beat/raise story
summary: GEV reports 7:00 AM ET Wed; Strazik/Parks webcast 7:30 AM. Stock +70% YTD as the AI-datacenter gas-turbine cycle blew past consensus. Slots for the flagship 7HA/9HA are sold out through 2030 per Strazik's May comments; investors focused on (1) 2026 revenue/EBITDA guide raise, (2) Power segment book-to-bill (was 1.7x Q1), (3) Grid segment margin trajectory (Q1 hit 11.5%, targeting mid-teens), (4) Wind segment EBITDA drag — mgmt guided $(200-300)M for H2 vs $(500-700)M FY25. Any commentary on the Meta/xAI 'behind-the-meter' turbine pipeline will move the tape.

headline: Big-week earnings kickoff: GEV/IBM Wed pre / TSLA + GOOGL Wed AH / INTC Thu AH / SAP Thu AH / AXP Fri — AI monetization vs capex-discipline the market's tell
summary: Bar has been reset lower after last week's SOX drawdown (-10%). Key setups: (1) GOOGL — Search AI-monetization proof + capex trajectory (Q1 $22B, guide $75B FY); (2) TSLA — auto margin + Robotaxi Austin metrics; (3) INTC 18A yields now 85% per mgmt, Data Center & AI needs to clear $5B for the turnaround narrative to hold (Polymarket 75.5% probability); (4) SAP cloud revenue consensus EUR 6.26B (+22% YoY), cloud backlog needs 23%+ to reassure. IBM software / consulting split the underappreciated Wed print.
